tools.name: create_invoice
tools.description: Generate an invoice. **Phase 1 supports Germany (DE) and the United States (US) only** — DE emits an EN 16931-compliant XRechnung (UBL by default, CII via format_override) or ZUGFeRD COMFORT, US emits a plain PDF. Any other sender jurisdiction is rejected with `unsupported_jurisdiction`. The format

tools.name: get_invoice
tools.description: Fetch a previously generated invoice by its `invoice_id` and return its current metadata plus a freshly signed download URL. Read-only and tenant-scoped — cross-tenant lookups return 404. Use this after `create_invoice` to re-download bytes from a different device, or to obtain a new signed URL afte

tools.name: list_supported_jurisdictions
tools.description: List jurisdictions Scribo can emit invoices for, with each jurisdiction's available formats and the default format applied when no override is given. Phase 1 returns Germany (DE — XRechnung / ZUGFeRD) and the United States (US — plain PDF) only. Read-only and side-effect-free; safe to call before `c

tools.name: verify_email_code
tools.description: Use this after `create_invoice` returns `verification_required`. Arguments: `{ challenge_id, code }`. On success returns a `verification_token`. Retry `create_invoice` with the same arguments plus that `verification_token` passed in its `verification_token` field. The token is reusable for ~30 min,

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "create_invoice" stacks urgency/override words (never, override). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Generate an invoice. **Phase 1 supports Germany (DE) and the United States (US) only** — DE emits an EN 16931-compliant XRechnung (UBL by default, CII via forma…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_invoice · Private data: create_invoice, verify_email_code · Public sink: create_invoice, verify_email_code · Destructive: create_invoice, list_supported_jurisdictions.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_invoice · Private data: create_invoice, verify_email_code · Public sink: create_invoice, verify_email_code · Destructive: create_invoice, list_supported_jurisdictions

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
